metadata.title:
LABOR CONVENTION PASSED


MANY VITAL RESOLUTIONS

pages.raw_text:
laws in the interest of the public
health or morals.


1.-THE Federation also protested
against the proposed tariff reduc-
ton on Philippine products and re-
quested Washington's senators and
Representatives to vote against
house bill No. 8.


The report of President Black
man was a splendid piece Of work
and contained many recommends
tions, among which was one advo-
eating the election of union men to
the state and municipal govern
ments, and that union men take
more interest in politics as such.
This recommendation was unanim-
ously concurred in. The seating of
fraternal delegates from the Farm
ers' Alliance and from the Minis
terial association of Seattle marks
an epoch in the labor movement of
the west, and both of these gentle
men in addresses in open meeting
confessed that they had in the con-
vention received a liberal education
along trade union lines, and would
forever be favorable to organized
labor, and would so report to their
respective associations. The State
Grange also elected a delegate, but
he was detained by illness. The
convention elected President Black
